channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The effects of the Trump administration’s efforts to roll back DEI initiatives
item.description: Coming up at 9 a.m. on Thursday, MPR News host Angela Davis and her guests talk about the steps President Donald Trump has taken to dismantle the federal government’s diversity, equity and inclusion programs and how some private companies have responded.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: U.S. immigration website stops accepting applicants for Biden program in Trump crackdown
item.description: The U.S. Immigration website recently blocked a form needed to sign up for a defunct Biden parole program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: National test scores show Texas students still lag in math and reading
item.description: Texas students' overall math scores dropped from 2022. But English learners and fourth-grade Black students did better than their peers elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison, KPRC 2

item.title: US children fall further behind in reading, make little improvement in math on national exam
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — America’s children have continued to lose ground on reading skills in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic and have made little improvement in math, according to the latest results of an exam known as the nation’s report card. The findings are yet another setback for U.S.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sec. Noem says Homeland Security will freeze grants to non-governmental organizations
item.description: DHS Secretary Kristi Noem announced Homeland Security will freeze grants to non-governmental organizations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meta agrees to pay $25 million to settle lawsuit from Trump after Jan. 6 suspension
item.description: Meta has agreed to pay $25 million to settle a lawsuit filed by President Donald Trump against the company after it suspended his accounts following the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Idaho House calls on Supreme Court to undo same-sex marriage ruling
item.description: The Supreme Court does not overturn its previous rulings absent a new case presenting a legal challenge.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Scoop: Key GOP senator who hesitated on Pete Hegseth pushes Kash Patel for FBI
item.description: Sen. Thom Tillis, R-N.C., is going all in on Kash Patel despite being a holdout on Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, whose confirmation came down to the wire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fetterman: RFK Jr.’s nomination in trouble after rocky hearing
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.), who was thought to be open to voting for Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s nomination to head the Department of Health and Human Services, now says the nominee is in serious trouble after his rocky confirmation hearing. “I don’t think it went well for him today.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House aide says spending freeze isn't being rescinded despite memo
item.description: The White House is claiming the Wednesday move by the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) rescinding a controversial order that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance is not actually an end to curbing government spending. “This is NOT a rescission of the federal funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is the Laken Riley Act? A look at the first bill Trump just signed
item.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday signed the first bill of his new administration, and it is named after a slain Georgia nursing student whose name became a rallying cry during his White House campaign.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's orders take aim at critical race theory and antisemitism on college campuses
item.description: President Donald Trump is ordering U.S. schools to stop teaching what he views as “critical race theory” and other material dealing with race and sexuality or risk losing their federal money.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump signs Laken Riley Act into law as first legislative victory in new administration
item.description: The Laken Riley Act directs Immigration and Customs Enforcement to detain illegal immigrants arrested or charged with theft-related crimes, or those accused of assaulting a police officer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Democratic Party in crisis will elect its new chairman on Saturday
item.description: The Democratic National Committee will elect a new chair Saturday as it tries to guide Democrats through Republican Donald Trump’s second presidency.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: White House rescinds federal funding freeze, defusing brewing legal battle
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has rescinded a plan for a sweeping pause on federal grants and loans totaling potentially trillions of dollars after it was temporarily blocked by a federal judge.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump appeals hush money criminal conviction
item.description: President Trump appealed his New York hush money criminal conviction and has brought on a new team of attorneys to represent him.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Harris team planned Houston rally with Joe Rogan in mind
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris made a substantial effort to get on Joe Rogan’s popular podcast amid concerns about her traction with male voters — going so far as to plan a Houston rally to justify a stop at Rogan’s Austin studio, according to a forthcoming book.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Defense secretary pulls Trump critic Gen. Milley's security clearance and protective detail
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is pulling the security protections and clearance of retired Joint Chiefs chairman Gen. Mark Milley.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump order aims to end federal support for gender transitions for those under 19
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aimed at cutting federal support for gender transitions for people under age 19, his latest move to roll back protections for transgender people across the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right recommendations on health issues
item.description: Fewer than half of Americans in a new poll say they trust President Trump or his health-related nominees to make recommendations on the nation's pressing health care issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Ousted IG: Trump firings a 'a potentially existential threat' to government oversight
item.description: One of 17 government watchdogs ousted by President Trump said that the move was a “a potentially existential threat” to a crucial government oversight tool.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump orders federal funding freeze: What it means for NYers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – President Donald Trump’s administration has issued a pause on all federal grants and loans, according to a memo from Matthew Vaeth, acting director of the Office of Management and Budget. The memo was sent on Monday but the freeze goes into effect Tuesday at 5 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Trump signs order that likely sets in motion a future ban on transgender troops
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order Monday directing Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to revise the Pentagon’s policy on transgender troops, likely setting in motion a future ban on their military service.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs executive order taking aim at DEI programs in the military
item.description: Trump said the diversity programs discourage merit and leadership, and discriminate on the basis of race and sex. The directive was among a series of orders that seeks to reshape the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie, MPR News

item.title: News for Trump: Denmark's last envoy to Greenland says U.K. may have first dibs
item.description: Denmark's last minister for Greenland says Britain once claimed right of first refusal over the territory — because of its proximity to Canada, which Britain once ruled.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer, MPR News

item.title: Voters to receive postcards for District 1 special primary election
item.description: Registered voters in San Diego County's First Supervisorial District will soon receive postcards for the April 8 special primary election.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Department moves to fire at least 12 officials who investigated Trump
item.description: In termination letters sent to more than a dozen officials, acting Attorney General James McHenry wrote that he did not believe they "could be trusted to faithfully implement the President's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas and Carrie Johnson, MPR News

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender people from military service
item.description: A fact sheet for Monday’s executive order refers to transgender Americans as “trans-identifying,” a technical term used by some to delegitimize transgender identities.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Florida Republicans end DeSantis special session before calling their own
item.description: Lawmakers in Tallahassee on Monday abruptly ended a special session called by Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis to address illegal immigration and other issues.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gabbard's path through Intelligence Committee narrows ahead of key hearing
item.description: Republicans are cautioning that Tulsi Gabbard’s path to confirmation to lead the U.S. intelligence apparatus is narrowing as she seemingly has trouble winning over key GOP senators. Gabbard, along with Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's move to freeze Biden-approved funding draws howls from Democrats
item.description: Democrats are sounding the alarm over President Trump's move to freeze funding approved in two of former President Biden's signature laws. Trump issued an executive order pausing the disbursement of funds appropriated through the Inflation Reduction Act and the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Nikki Fried vows to rebuild Florida Democrats as re-elected chair
item.description: Nikki Fried was reelected Saturday as chair of the Florida Democratic Party, which is seeking to regain relevance in a state that has tilted decidedly toward the Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida schools will comply with ICE on campuses: Education department
item.description: Schools across America, including in Florida, are gearing up for the possibility of officials with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) showing up to their campuses.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: US active duty troops beginning to arrive in Texas, San Diego to support border security
item.description: Active duty military troops will begin arriving in El Paso, Texas, and San Diego on Thursday evening, in what defense officials said is the first batch of the new forces being deployed to secure the southern border.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Early favorite emerges in Republican primary for NJ governor: poll
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – A candidate has emerged as an early favorite in the Republican primary for New Jersey governor, a new Emerson College Polling/PIX11/The Hill poll shows.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11
